steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Identify the area formula for a rectangle
steps.content: The area of a rectangle is given by $A = \text{length} \times \text{width}$. We are given length = 56 inches and width = 28 inches.

steps.title: Multiply to find the area
steps.content:
$$A = 56 \times 28 = 1,568$$
The area is 1,568 square inches. That confirms D is correct.
